title: 三只瞎老鼠
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\151-三只瞎老鼠-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Mr. Satterthwaite experiences a moment of intense intuition and paranoia during a tea service, sensing an impending crisis. He observes Beryl Gillett at the table, noting that she accidentally breaks a red teacup and replaces it with a light blue one from a tray, repositioning an opalite pipe next to the new cups. When Timothy approaches the table, Inez mistakenly identifies the blue cup as hers and the red one as his, but Timothy insists the cup with sugar is his.

Sensing danger, Mr. Satterthwaite rushes forward and shouts for Timothy not to drink. He explains to a bewildered Dr. Hodton that Timothy is color blind and was deceived by the cup swap. During this confrontation, Mr. Satterthwaite reveals a realization regarding parentage: he claims Timothy is Lily’s son, while Roland is the son of Bertrill and Christopher Eden. Mr. Satterthwaite alleges that a woman intentionally purchased the specific teacups to facilitate a poisoning, knowing Timothy could not distinguish the colors.

Dr. Hodton takes the blue cup from Timothy under the guise of examining a flaw in the porcelain. As the men walk toward the gate, Beryl Gillett rushes toward the house, appearing anxious and fearful. She encounters Tom Addison and sharply points out that he is wearing mismatched shoes—one red and one green—noting that he cannot see the difference. Beryl then flees the scene. As Mr. Satterthwaite and Dr. Hodton watch a motorcycle speed away, Mr. Satterthwaite concludes that Beryl's departure is likely for the best.
